sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 21.34 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.21)

statement.latex: (Well-known problem). A group$G$is a if, for any nonempty finite subsets$A, B$of$G$, there exists an element of$G$which can be written uniquely as$ab$with$a A$and$b B$. A group$G$is if$G$admits a partial order$<$such that for all$g, h G$with$h 1$, we have either$gh > g$or$gh^{-1} >g$. Does there exist a unique product group which is not locally invariant orderable?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
